evidence_quotes.quote: there is no paragraph requiring ‘Life Service Games’ to submit an ‘end of live plan’ in order to remain playable in video games, e.g. private servers or an offline function, even after being switched off from the main servers and/or online mode.

an obligation on large social networks such as Meta or X Platformrat(s) to install civil society and/or experts in company decisions, such as the Facebook Oversight Board or the Social Media Council in Ireland under Article 19.

paragraphs.text: dear European Commission, I would give my feedback on the Digital Fairness Act (DFA). 1 there is no paragraph requiring ‘Life Service Games’ to submit an ‘end of live plan’ in order to remain playable in video games, e.g. private servers or an offline function, even after being switched off from the main servers and/or online mode. 2 an obligation on large social networks such as Meta or X Platformrat(s) to install civil society and/or experts in company decisions, such as the Facebook Oversight Board or the Social Media Council in Ireland under Article 19.
